2603.25613.title: Demographic Fairness in Multimodal LLMs: A Benchmark of Gender and Ethnicity Bias in Face Verification
2603.25613.paper_summary: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have recently been explored as face verification systems that determine whether two face images are of the same person. Unlike dedicated face recognition systems, MLLMs approach this task through visual prompting and rely on general visual and reasoning abilities. However, the demographic fairness of these models remains largely unexplored. In this paper, we present a benchmarking study that evaluates nine open-source MLLMs from six model families, ranging from 2B to 8B parameters, on the IJB-C and RFW face verification protocols across four ethnicity groups and two gender groups. We measure verification accuracy with the Equal Error Rate and True Match Rate at multiple operating points per demographic group, and we quantify demographic disparity with four FMR-based fairness metrics. Our results show that FaceLLM-8B, the only face-specialised model in our study, substantially outperforms general-purpose MLLMs on both benchmarks. The bias patterns we observe differ from those commonly reported for traditional face recognition, with different groups being most affected depending on the benchmark and the model. We also note that the most accurate models are not necessarily the fairest and that models with poor overall accuracy can appear fair simply because they produce uniformly high error rates across all demographic groups.
2603.25613.paper_summary_zh: 多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）最近被探索作為面部驗證系統，以確定兩張面部圖像是否為同一個人。與專用的面部識別系統不同，MLLMs 通過視覺提示來處理這一任務，並依賴於一般的視覺和推理能力。然而，這些模型的人口公平性仍然在很大程度上未被探索。在本文中，我們呈現了一項基準研究，評估來自六個模型家族的九個開源 MLLMs，參數範圍從 2B 到 8B，並在 IJB-C 和 RFW 面部驗證協議中針對四個種族群體和兩個性別群體進行評估。我們通過平等錯誤率和真實匹配率在每個人口群體的多個操作點上測量驗證準確性，並通過四個基於 FMR 的公平性指標量化人口差異。我們的結果顯示，FaceLLM-8B，作為我們研究中唯一的面部專用模型，在這兩個基準上明顯超越了通用 MLLMs。我們觀察到的偏見模式與傳統面部識別中常見的報告有所不同，受影響最嚴重的不同群體取決於基準和模型。我們還注意到，最準確的模型不一定是最公平的，而整體準確性差的模型可能僅因為在所有人口群體中產生均勻的高錯誤率而顯得公平。
2603.25613.author: Ünsal Öztürk et.al.
2603.25613.authors: Ünsal Öztürk, Hatef Otroshi Shahreza, Sébastien Marcel
